title: How to Build a Simple Net Worth Habit
blurb: A 20-minute quarterly ritual that makes your balance sheet trend visible.
meta: Track net worth in 20 minutes a quarter. What to include, what to skip, and how the trend matters more than one number.

title: Needs, Wants, and the Gray Zone
blurb: Classify spending honestly when 50/30/20 meets real life.
meta: Make 50/30/20 work in real life. How to classify spending when categories blur—and how to fix a needs-heavy budget.

title: The True Cost of Minimum Payments
blurb: Why minimums prolong high-APR debt—and how fixed payoffs change the math.
meta: Why minimum payments keep you in debt for years and how fixed payoff amounts change interest and time.

title: Fixed vs Variable Rate Loans
blurb: Payment certainty versus rate risk, with a simple stress test.
meta: Payment certainty versus rate risk. How to stress-test a variable rate and choose with eyes open.

title: Down Payments: 5%, 10%, or 20%?
blurb: Cash today vs interest over time—and why reserves still matter.
meta: How down payment size changes payment, interest, cash reserves, and risk—not just “how soon can we buy.”

title: Renting Longer Isn’t Always “Throwing Money Away”
blurb: When renting is the rational financial choice.
meta: When renting is financially rational: flexibility, transaction costs, short horizons, and opportunity cost.

title: What to Do With a Windfall (Bonus, Tax Refund, Gift)
blurb: Bonus and refund order of operations without lifestyle creep.
meta: A calm order of operations for bonuses and tax refunds—debt, buffer, goals, then investing.

title: Index Funds in Plain English
blurb: Diversification, fees, and realistic expectations—no product hype.
meta: What an index fund is, why fees matter, and how diversification works—without product hype.

title: Risk Tolerance vs Risk Capacity
blurb: Feelings about risk vs ability to absorb losses.
meta: Feelings about volatility are not the same as the ability to absorb losses. Align investing with both.

title: Sequence of Returns Risk (Why Early Retirement Is Harder)
blurb: Why average returns mislead in early retirement.
meta: Why average returns mislead early retirees and how bad markets at the start of withdrawals change outcomes.

title: How Much House Is Too Much?
meta: Payment comfort, maintenance, and opportunity cost—why lender maximum is not the same as enough life left over.

title: Car Buying: Cash, Loan, or Wait
blurb: Total cost of ownership and financing traps.
meta: Depreciation, total cost of ownership, and when financing is tool versus trap.

title: Student Loans After Graduation: First 90 Days
blurb: Inventory, cash flow, and strategy after graduation.
meta: A practical first-three-months checklist: inventory loans, build a budget, and choose a payment approach.

title: Insurance Basics: What Gap Are You Actually Filling?
blurb: Coverage as a goals inventory before you talk to an advisor.
meta: Life insurance as a goals inventory, not a fear purchase. How to use a gap estimate before you talk to a licensed advisor.

title: A One-Hour Monthly Money Review
blurb: A repeatable monthly agenda for households.
meta: A repeatable 60-minute agenda: accounts, budget variance, debts, goals, and a net-worth note.
